_**User**_

Search this codebase for phase documentation, roadmap, or planning docs related to registration features. Look for:
- Phase 8, phase 9, or similar phase docs
- Role-based access (reception officer, population officer, police clerk)
- Case locking, assignment, audit trail
- Any mention of "reception", "population", "police", "lock", "assign"

Search in: .specstory/, docs/, README, any markdown files, and project planning files.

Return: file paths, relevant excerpts about phases and these features, and whether case access control / locking / audit is planned for a later phase.

---

## Primary planning documents

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/docs/ROADMAP.md` | Master phased plan (Phases 0–11) |
| `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/docs/phases/README.md` | Index of completed phase delivery notes |
| `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/docs/phases/phase-*.md` | Detailed notes for Phases 0–7 (complete) |
| `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/IDEA.md` | Original process walkthrough (different phase numbering) |
| `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/docs/features/registration/README.md` | Registration slice index + status/role behavior |
| `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/docs/GLOSSARY.md` | Officer role definitions |
| `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Auth, audit, authorization conventions |
| `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/README.md` | Design principles (includes audit) |

**SpecStory session notes** (planning/discussion, not canonical specs):
- `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/.specstory/history/2026-07-05_17-31-49Z-phase-8-overview.md`
- `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/.specstory/history/2026-07-05_18-23-47Z-difference-between-officer-roles.md`
- `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/.specstory/history/2026-07-05_18-18-58Z-registration-feature-discussion.md`

---

## Phase 8, 9, and beyond

### Phase 8 — Certificates & outbound stubs (Planned)

From `docs/ROADMAP.md`:

> **Goal:** Citizen-facing outputs and administration notifications.
>
> **Slices:** `IssueResidenceCertificate`, `IssueHouseholdComposition`, `ListOutboundNotifications`
>
> **Infrastructure:** PDF generation stub; notification log table
>
> **Demo:** Registration complete → certificate PDF → log shows “Notify tax administration.”

`docs/phases/README.md` lists Phase 8 as **Planned** with no delivery doc yet. Git status shows Phase 8 work may be in progress in code.

**Naming collision:** In `IDEA.md`, “Phase 8” is **Civil Status** — already delivered as Roadmap Phase 4 (`RecordCivilStatus`).

### Phase 9 — Exception scenarios (Planned)

From `docs/ROADMAP.md`:

> **Goal:** Teach edge cases as explicit workflows.
>
> | Feature | Priority |
> | Missing birth certificate → suspend | High |
> | Marriage not recognised | Medium |
> | Duplicate identity investigation flag | High |
> | Illegal stay → reject + refer | Medium |
> | Refugee / temporary protection path | Medium |
> | Diplomat separate rules | Low |
> | Homeless reference address | Low |
> | EU vs non-EU policy differences (deepen) | High |

Phase 7 doc notes: *“Phase 9: exception scenarios hook into reject/suspend reasons.”*

### Phase 10 — PostgreSQL hardening & Aspire deployment (Planned)

Infrastructure only: Testcontainers coverage, snake_case, OpenTelemetry, health checks, `aspire publish`, OpenAPI. **No new registration access-control features.**

### Phase 11 — Extended municipality (Optional)

Change of address, birth declaration, passport/ID workflow, FR/NL UI, reporting dashboard, consolidated **Person file** read model.

---

## Role-based access (reception / population / police)

### Documented roles

From `docs/GLOSSARY.md`:

| Term | Code | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| **Reception officer** | `ReceptionOfficer` | Front desk; determines visit reason and routing |
| **Population officer** | `PopulationOfficer` | Handles registration, identity, address, decision |
| **Police clerk** | `PoliceClerk` | Records residence verification outcomes |

### When roles were introduced

**Phase 0** (`docs/phases/phase-0-foundation.md`):

> `OfficerRole`: `ReceptionOfficer`, `PopulationOfficer`, `PoliceClerk`
>
> `ICurrentOfficer` — current officer id, display name, role, `CanApproveRegistration`
>
> **Demo:** Role switcher — change between Reception, Population, and Police

**Phase 6** (`docs/ROADMAP.md` / `docs/phases/phase-6-police-verification-loop.md`):

> **Goal:** Async residence check with separate police role.
>
> - Population officer: “Send to police” action on case detail
> - Police clerk portal: pending list, result form
> - Nav badge with pending count (police clerk role)

### Current enforcement (from `.specstory/history/2026-07-05_18-23-47Z-difference-between-officer-roles.md`)

> **Population officer only:** `CanApproveRegistration` gates approve/reject/suspend/confirm; Decision section and Outbound notifications nav restricted.
>
> **Reception officer:** No handler or UI checks reference `ReceptionOfficer` except the role switcher — *“reflects the intended future ‘Reception’ bounded context more than an enforced restriction today.”*
>
> **Police clerk:** Records police results; police verification list visible to all, but recording restricted to Police clerk.

**Real auth** deferred in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`:

> Phase 1: role picker or fixed test users. Phase 2+: ASP.NET Core Identity or OpenID Connect stub.

---

## Case assignment

**Implemented in Phase 1** — not a future phase.

From `docs/ROADMAP.md` Phase 1:

> New case dialog (visit reason, **assign officer**)

From `docs/DOMAIN.md`:

> `AssignedOfficerId` — Population officer

From `docs/features/registration/open-registration-case.md`:

> `OpenRegistrationCaseRequest(VisitReason, Guid? AssignedOfficerId)` — when null, current officer from `ICurrentOfficer` is used.

This is **ownership metadata at case open**, not exclusive locking for concurrent editing.

---

## Audit trail

### Planned and delivered in Phase 7

From `docs/phases/phase-2.1-intake-corrections.md`:

> Full `CaseAuditEntry` collection is **Phase 7 scope**. Phase 2.1 should log corrections at the application layer (structured log).

From `docs/phases/phase-7-decision-registration.md`:

> A persistent **case audit trail** records decisions; `RegistrationConfirmed` domain event triggers stub outbound notifications.
>
> | `CaseAuditEntry` + `ListCaseAudit` | Done | Persistent audit table |
>
> **Demo:** Case history timeline shows approve + confirm entries.
>
> **Test:** `RejectCase_PreservesAuditTrail`

From `docs/ROADMAP.md` Phase 7:

> Rejection path preserves audit trail

From `README.md` design principles:

> **Audit everything that matters** — who changed what, when, and why

From `IDEA.md` (person file vision):

> Historical changes and audit trail

**Audit is not deferred** — it was explicitly scoped to Phase 7 and documented as complete.

---

## “Lock” — what exists vs. what is planned

**No roadmap phase describes exclusive case locking** (checkout, claim, concurrent-edit prevention).

What “lock/block” means in docs:

1. **UI lock after save (Phase 2.1 motivation)** — `docs/ROADMAP.md`:
   > Phases 1–2 only support first-time recording; **the UI locks sections after save**.

2. **Status-based edit guards** — `docs/features/registration/README.md`:
   > `Approved`, `Registered`, `Rejected` → **Blocked** — terminal or post-decision
   >
   > Domain guard: `EnsureIntakeDataEditable()` (`Intake` + `UnderReview`)

3. **Police-wait blocking** — `docs/phases/phase-6-police-verification-loop.md`:
   > Intake edits remain blocked while status is `AwaitingPoliceVerification`

These are **lifecycle guards**, not officer-level case locks.

---

## Is case access control / locking / audit planned for a later phase?

| Feature | Status in docs |
|---------|----------------|
| **Audit trail** | **Phase 7** — planned and documented as **complete** (`CaseAuditEntry`, `ListCaseAudit`, timeline UI) |
| **Role-based access** | **Partially done** — fake auth since Phase 0; Population + Police enforced; Reception is placeholder; real auth mentioned for “Phase 2+” in ARCHITECTURE but not on ROADMAP |
| **Case assignment** | **Phase 1** — `AssignedOfficerId` at open; not expanded in later phases |
| **Exclusive case locking** | **Not planned** in ROADMAP Phases 8–11 or any phase doc found |
| **Reception bounded context** | **Future aspiration** only (SpecStory discussion); no phase number |
| **Stronger RBAC** | Not explicitly scheduled; Phase 10 is infra; Phase 11 is new workflows |

---

## Relevant excerpts by topic

**Reception workflow (`IDEA.md`):**
> The reception officer determines the reason for the visit… For a foreign citizen registering for the first time, the reception officer directs them to Population/Foreigners Office.

**Police loop (`docs/ROADMAP.md` Phase 6):**
> **Goal:** Async residence check with separate police role.

**Phase 8 carry-forward (`docs/phases/phase-7-decision-registration.md`):**
> - Phase 8: `IssueResidenceCertificate`, `ListOutboundNotifications` (replace logging handler)
> - Phase 9: exception scenarios hook into reject/suspend reasons

**Post–Phase 8 architecture (`registration-feature-discussion.md`):**
> Phase 11-style workflows may split `Features/Registration/` into `PoliceVerification`, `Certificates`, `Notifications`, etc. Phase 10 is mostly infrastructure — no new feature folders.

---

**Bottom line:** Phases 8–11 focus on certificates, exceptions, infrastructure, and extended municipality workflows. **Audit trail was a Phase 7 deliverable.** **Role separation** exists for Population (decisions) and Police (verification), with Reception still conceptual. **Case locking for concurrent access is not documented as a planned feature** anywhere in the searched planning files.
